title: Capaciteitsmanager Business Clients & Ecosystems
description:
In het kort
Ben jij die ervaren Capaciteitsmanager die workforce management binnen onze afdeling naar een hoger niveau wil brengen? Ben je een ervaren WFM-er, slim, ambitieus, analytisch sterk en communicatief vaardig?  Weet jij als geen ander hoe je interne stakeholders moet overtuigen van jouw adviezen en bevindingen? Dan gaan wij graag met jou in gesprek!
Je werk
Je werkt in een klein WFM team en bent daar verantwoordelijk voor de WFM cyclus en het forecastproces bij Business Clients. Je maakt de forecast voor alle werkstromen die met name via Microsoft Dynamics lopen en onderzoekt hoe we in de toekomst alle werkzaamheden van de business kunnen forcecasten en inplannen via WFM. Je stelt een capaciteitsplan op en geeft het management aan de hand hiervan advies over benodigde capaciteit en werkverdeling. De uitkomsten van het capaciteitsoverleg draag je over aan de Planners en Traffic managers. Je levert een proactieve bijdrage aan de inzet en optimalisatie van WFM. Je rapporteert aan de manager WFM BC&E. Het betreft een fulltime functie.
Werkomgeving
Bij het Centre of Expertise (CoE) WFM zijn alle WFM experts binnen de bank ondergebracht die voor verschillende afdelingen WFM neerzetten en uitvoeren. Je komt te werken in het executie team dat verantwoordelijk is voor WFM bij Business Clients & Ecosystems (BC&E).
Je standplaats wordt de Wisselwerking 58 in Diemen maar bij het Centre of Expertise WFM werken wij hybride, hierbij bepalen de werkzaamheden wat voor jou die dag de beste werkplek is.
Business Clients & Ecosystems
De client unit Business Clients & Ecosystems (BC&E) is verantwoordelijk voor het bedienen van zakelijke klanten met een omzet tot 25 miljoen Euro, ingedeeld in 'sectorclusters'. De focus van BC&E ligt op het behalen van onze groeidoelstelling om de nummer één bank voor het midden- en kleinbedrijf  (zakelijke klanten met omzet tot 25 miljoen) te worden.
BC&E bedient +/- 370.000 zakelijke klanten (inclusief Medical Business Clients). De afdeling adviseert zakelijke klanten over dagelijkse zakelijke producten en diensten, zoals leningen tot een miljoen Euro, betaaloplossingen, sparen, verzekeringen, beyond en near banking proposities en ondersteuning bij het dagelijks bankieren.
Je profiel
Meerdere jaren werkervaring op het gebied van Workforce management (WFM); Ervaring met het implementeren van WFM bij een nieuwe afdeling is een pre.
Analytisch sterk en communicatief vaardig.
Kennis en ervaring met PowerBI rapportages is een pre.
Wij bieden
Het is voor ons belangrijk om een goede werkgever te zijn. Wij bieden je daarom een functie met impact én uitstekende arbeidsvoorwaarden die bijdragen aan jouw werkplezier, ontwikkeling en wellbeing.
Daarnaast bieden wij:
Een aantrekkelijk bruto maandsalaris op basis van een 36urige werkweek. Dit is inclusief vakantiegeld en een flexibel benefit budget.
Een uitstekende pensioenregeling, zodat je goed voorbereid bent voor later.
Flexibiliteit in werken: thuiswerken is mogelijk in overleg met je team en afhankelijk van je functie. Wij regelen een ergonomische thuiswerkplek voor je.
Ruimte voor ontspanning met vijf weken vakantie per jaar, aangevuld met twee verplichte vrije dagen. Daarnaast kun je jaarlijks tot vier extra weken vakantie bijkopen.
Vijf ‘Banking for better days’: extra vrije dagen die je kunt inzetten voor persoonlijke ontwikkeling of vrijwilligerswerk.
Persoonlijke ontwikkeling staat centraal: je ontvangt een ontwikkelbudget van €1.000 per jaar, dat je kunt opsparen tot €3.000.
Een OV-jaarabonnement met gratis openbaar vervoer in heel Nederland voor zakelijk en privé gebruik.
Interesse?
Heb je interesse? Reageer dan door middel van een sollicitatie. Voor extra inhoudelijke info graag even reageren naar
thomas.van.hassel@nl.abnamro.com
/ 06 1439 7379.
Jouw Toekomst: Inclusief, Innovatief en Duurzaam
Bij ABN AMRO geloven we in "
Banking for better, for generations to come.
" Gelijke kansen voor iedereen vormen een belangrijk fundament, waarbij we streven naar een inclusieve cultuur waarin alle medewerkers zich gezien, gehoord en gewaardeerd voelen. Onze visie als
persoonlijke bank in de digitale tijd
sluit naadloos aan bij de behoefte aan verrassende inzichten en innovatieve oplossingen, voortkomend uit een divers samenspel van culturen en ervaringen. We richten ons op klantbeleving, duurzaamheid en bouwen aan een toekomstbestendige bank, waarbij we gelijke beloning voor gelijk werk jaarlijks onderzoeken om eerlijke kansen te waarborgen. Sluit je aan bij een bank die vindingrijkheid en ambitie omarmt, en maak samen met ons impact voor een betere toekomst.
Als je wilt solliciteren voor het B-Able of Reboot programma, zorg er dan voor dat je dit vermeldt zowel in de titel als in de inhoud van je cv.

title: Senior Data Analyst
description:
At a glance
Are you passionate about both advanced analytics and empowering others to grow in their analytical capabilities? Do you have the expertise to lead by example while fostering a culture of statistical thinking? If so, we have the perfect role for you!
Channel Security is seeking a Lead Data Analyst to join our team; Fraud Monitoring. In this role, you will not only be a technical expert and a strong individual contributor but also a key leader in advancing the analytics maturity of a team with a business-driven background. You will serve as a pillar for analytics standards and help shape the way we use data to make impactful decisions.
Your job
As a Lead Data Analyst within the Fraud Monitoring team, you will play a central role in ensuring that our analysis and detection logic are grounded in solid reasoning and supported by evidence. You will work closely with Fraud Experts (FEs), Data Scientists, and Machine Learning Engineers to strengthen our fraud detection capabilities across payment channels to catch frauds such as phishing, account takeover, lost or stolen cards, and other emerging threats.
Your work focuses on deep‑dive analysis, understanding fraud behaviour, evaluating the effectiveness of detection logic, and validating whether proposed changes are analytically sound. You will help the team distinguish clear signals from noise, recognise when evidence is insufficient, and ensure our decisions are based on robust data rather than anecdotal cases.
This is a hands‑on analytical role with significant influence: your insights and your methodological judgment will directly shape how we detect fraud and protect our customers.
Key Responsibilities:
Analytical Excellence
Perform in‑depth analyses to understand fraud patterns, attack vectors, seasonality, behavioural shifts, and detection performance across payment channels.
Evaluate the strength of proposed detection logic (features, thresholds, rules) and identify when ideas are overly narrow, unsupported by evidence, or prone to false positives.
Assess the value and reliability of data fields and signals used in fraud detection.
Help ensure our reasoning, conclusions, and decisions are analytically sound and well‑supported.
Methodological Leadership
Guide Fraud Experts and junior analysts in applying good analytical practices and critical thinking.
Coach colleagues on interpreting data responsibly, avoiding common analytical pitfalls, and understanding basic statistical concepts such as variance, sample bias, and signal strength.
Promote a culture of analytical rigor — ensuring the team adopts more mature, data‑driven ways of working.
Collaboration & Impact
Partner with Data Scientists to transform analytical insights into hypotheses, experiments, and feature ideas for model development.
Act as a bridge between domain experts and technical experts, ensuring clear understanding on both sides.
Identify opportunities to improve how we measure fraud impact, effectiveness of controls, and operational efficiency.
Drive improvements in how we use data in the team — from investigation workflows to decision support tools — focusing on quality, clarity, and insight rather than dashboard volume.
Working environment
Grid Channel Security within Corporate Information Security Office (CISO) is responsible for the prevention and detection of payment fraud, and deciding the policy and strategy in the area of fraud. The Fraud Monitoring team that you are going to be part of, is a motivated multidisciplinary group of approximately 25 colleagues working with a focus on protecting customers. We work in a hybrid way and our day to day work is organized by the agile and scrum principles. CISO is recognised as having an informal work atmosphere where everyone has a heart for their job and is prepared to help others.
Your profile
Qualifications and technical skills:
A master’s degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Computer Science, Statistics, Econometrics, Engineering, Physics, Mathematics, or similar).
At least 3 years of experience in an analytical role involving hands-on data exploration, problem solving, and interpretation (e.g., data analyst, fraud analyst, risk analyst, business analyst with strong technical skills, data scientists in analytical track).
Technical skills:
Strong SQL skills for exploratory analysis and investigation.
Hands-on experience in Python for data analysis.
PySpark or experience working with large datasets.
Basic familiarity with visualization tools for communication — not for dashboard engineering.
Ability to frame analytical questions, interpret results correctly, and identify flawed logic or insufficient evidence.
Familiarity with basic statistical concepts (e.g., distributions, variance, seasonality, sampling, simple backtesting principles). Not as a statistician— but as a critical thinker.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.
Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
About you
You are an energetic Firestarter, proactively looking for and finding relevant data analysis questions
Strong critical thinking and the confidence to challenge weak assumptions.
Ability to clearly articulate reasoning, uncertainties, and trade-offs.
A Teacher and Mentor with a deep passion for upskilling and helping your fellow team members grow their analytical capabilities, especially guiding them to adopt statistical rigor during their analytical work.
Experience and understanding of Scrum and Agile methodologies, with the ability to apply these frameworks effectively in project management and team collaboration
We are offering
Flexibility in a hybrid work environment to support strong work-life balance;
An industry-competitive salary based on a 36 or 40-hour work week;
A supplementary benefit budget of 11%, which you can spend on pre-tax fringe benefits;
A personal development budget of EUR 1.000 per year;
An annual public transportation pass;
A solid pension plan.
Interested?
Does this opportunity sound like something you’d be interested in? Please send us your application as soon as possible. We look forward to meeting you.
Please note: This vacancy is a repost of a position previously published on 27 November 2025; if you have already received a rejection for this role, reapplying will not be considered.
Your Future: Inclusive, Innovative, Sustainable
At ABN AMRO, we believe in "
Banking for better, for generations to come.
" Equal opportunities for everyone are a crucial foundation, as we strive for an inclusive culture where all employees feel seen, heard, and valued. Our vision of
being a personal bank in the digital age
aligns perfectly with the demand for surprising insights and innovative solutions, born from a diverse interplay of cultures and experiences. We focus on customer experience, sustainability, and building a future-proof bank, conducting annual reviews to ensure equal pay for equal work. Join a bank that embraces ingenuity and ambition, and make an impact with us for a better future.
If you want to apply for the B-Able or Reboot program, make sure to mention it both in the title and the content of your resume.
Disclaimer external recruitment agencies
External recruitment agencies need to have a signed agreement with ABN AMRO BANK N.V., executed by a Talent Acquisition Specialist, when submitting a resume to a vacancy. No unsolicited services or offers, please.

match.llm_rationale: The resume shows strong technical AI/ML skills but lacks direct experience in fraud analysis, financial domains, or data analyst roles, with minimal overlap in key responsibilities like fraud pattern analysis, detection logic evaluation, and methodological leadership required for this senior data analyst position.

title: Data & AI Strategy Advisor
description:
In het kort
Do you want to lead the data & AI revolution? Do you have a strong passion for data & AI and believe that these are at the core of unlocking value for customers? Do you get energised by the state of the art in a complex environment? Then come and help us unlock the power of data & AI at the Central Data Office (CDO) of ABN AMRO!
Je werk
You directly support the execution of the ABN AMRO strategy: a Personal Bank in a Digital Age. You are part of the Data & AI Strategy & Design team within the Central Data Office. You set the strategic direction, shape new concepts, and drive complex, bank‑wide initiatives at the intersection of data, AI, regulation and business strategy. You have a diverse stakeholder field, spanning across technical and business areas as well leadership and operational levels.
Key responsibilities include:
Maintain and further develop the bank‑wide AI Strategy.
Shape strategic direction and state of the art on topics such as:
AI‑ready data (data quality, metadata, lineage, governance‑by‑design)
AI technologies, with a strong focus on its potential and responsible usage.
Define operating models, roles and responsibilities to translate the strategy into execution.
Spot and assess external trends and internal drivers for change, including developments in AI, data regulation and technology.
Act as a trusted sparring partner for the Head of Data & AI Governance, the Chief Data Officer and senior leadership.
Lead and support transformational initiatives (e.g. AI Strategy execution).
Ensure strategic recommendations are translated into concrete actions, roadmaps and decisions, and actively followed through to implementation.
Prepare and support executive decision‑making for the Executive Board and Supervisory Board.
Werkomgeving
You will work within the Central Data Office – Data and AI Governance – Strategy & Design team, closely collaborating with other CDO teams, IT teams, data domains, business lines, international branches and subsidiaries. We are in constant collaboration with all business lines, international branches and subsidiaries of the bank. Together, we operate at the forefront of data and AI in banking, working with some of ABN AMRO’s most critical value assets.
We work in an open and transparent environment, where your opinion is valued and dialogue is encouraged. The team is diverse with regards to culture, experience and age. The atmosphere is lively and informal, closely monitoring a safe environment where everyone can share their thoughts, feelings and worries. It is normal to make mistakes, and we aim to help each other grow better as learning is one of our main focuses.
Je profiel
You are a senior, execution‑oriented strategiser. You thrive in ambiguity and bridges strategy and delivery, backed by a strong background in consulting, driving complex programmes, particularly on AI. You can quickly master new topics, form clear strategic opinions, and translate strategy into executable change within a complex organisation.
Experience & skills
Master’s degree.
Proven experience in AI, in addition to Analytics and Data Management.
Strong consulting background, with experience advising senior stakeholders.
Experience leading enterprise‑wide data or AI initiatives.
Familiar with frameworks and regulations such as AI Act, DAMA‑DMBOK, Data Mesh, BCBS239, or similar.
Strong analytical, conceptual and communication skills.
What truly distinguishes you:
You learn new topics quickly and form clear, balanced strategic opinions tailored to executive audiences.
You naturally connect external trends to the internal context of ABN AMRO.
You can navigate organisational complexity and tailor solutions to different stakeholders and maturity levels.
You go beyond advice proactively: you turn recommendations into action and ensure follow‑through to implementation.
Wij bieden
We believe it is important to be a good employer. Joining us means stepping into a role with impact, supported by excellent employment conditions that foster your job satisfaction, development, and wellbeing.
In addition, we offer:
An attractive gross monthly salary based on a 36hour work week, including holiday allowance and a flexible benefit budget.
An excellent pension scheme, ensuring that you are well prepared for the future.
Flexibility in working: working from home is possible in consultation with your team and depending on your role. We will provide an ergonomic home office setup for you.
Plenty of room for relaxation with five weeks of vacation per year, supplemented by two mandatory days off. You can also purchase up to four additional weeks of vacation annually.
Five “Banking for better days”: extra days off that you can use for personal development or volunteer work.
Personal development is key: you receive a development budget of €1,000 per year, which can accumulate up to €3,000.
An annual public transport pass with free public transportation throughout the Netherlands for both business and private use.
You can find more information about our employment conditions and salary scales in our Collective Labour Agreement (CLA), which applies to this vacancy.
Interesse?
Please apply!

title: Data Analyst
description:
At a glance
Do you want to be part of the Data Revolution in the banking industry? Do you have a passion for data and do you agree that data is at the core of creating value for customers? Does working in a complex environment on data related cases make you happy? Then come help us unlock the power of data at the Enterprise Enabling Data Domain in Change Management & Consultancy (I&T) in ABN AMRO!
Your job
As a Data Analyst, your behaviour sets you apart. Alongside your technical expertise, you are able to collaborate and communicate effectively. You are comfortable to work in a fast-paced and dynamic environment and are curious to uncover the “why” behind every question. Your self-starting attitude allows you to embrace ambiguity and to think big while paying careful attention to details. In challenging situations, you are reliable, dedicated and able to think in solutions.
Your primary focus is on serving the interests of our internal and external stakeholders. As a Data Analyst, together with your team, you are responsible for the design, development, maintenance and continuous improvement of our data lineage- and analytics capabilities, as well as data visualization using business intelligence tools. You identify, analyse and interpret trends and patterns in and provide insights based upon data to further improve our operations, processes and strategy. You communicate findings effectively and translate them into recommended actions. To achieve all this, you work in close collaboration with data engineers and data stewards within your domain and various other stakeholders.
Some examples of your responsibilities:
•    Collaborating with stakeholders to understand their requirements and translating these into data-driven solutions
•    Driving continuous improvement of our business and IT processes by applying data analytics
•    Communicating your findings to stakeholders and proposing recommended actions
•    Finding new and innovative ways to analyse data together with your team
Working environment
As a Data Analyst in our team, you will be part of the BI & Analytics Centre of Excellence, a team that is responsible for the delivery of different types of reports and analyses, servicing a wide range of stakeholders across the bank. The atmosphere in the team is informal and energetic, and we’re ok with making ‘mistakes’ as long as everyone is open about it and learns from it. We all work together at the frontier of a major digital revolution and with the core value-assets (data!) of the organization. BI & Analytics therefore is in constant collaboration with business users, data providers, platform teams and many other teams to improve the delivery of their products.
The BI & Analytics team consists of approximately 20 Data Analysts and is part of the Enterprise Enabling Data Domain. We work alongside the Data Products teams, consisting of Data Management Experts, and the Data Science Centre of Excellence, consisting of Data Scientists. The team services MT I&T and underlying departments like the CDO, Procurement and the CIO's, but also the COO and multiple bank wide projects.
The Enterprise Enabling Data Domain consists of talented data professionals that collaborate in projects and programs based on the capabilities that are required. We fully support the development of our colleagues and facilitate opportunities that support this development. Autonomy and flexibility are two of many characteristics of the team. We are situated at the Zuidas in Amsterdam, but will soon move to Diemen temporarily. Nowadays we are of course fully into Hybrid Working.
Your profile
You are an analytical thinker, a do-er, whose behaviour reflects the culture principles of courage, care and collaboration. It is very important that you are able to translate business challenges to data solutions, like a true Data Evangelist. You have a passion for statistics and use this to gain valuable insights from data! You see relationships and translate these to new insights while maintaining a clear overview. Working with others inspires you to come up with new ideas and you are excited to share your insights with multiple stakeholders and data specialists!
This is on your resume:
•    Minimum of 2 years of experience in a relevant field
•    Consultancy experience and/or mindset, you’re asking the right questions to provide the insights needed and improve our business
•    Up-to-date on new data analysis methods
•    Knowledge and experience of BI tools like PowerBI, preferably with a PL-300 licence
•    Knowledge and experience in Python, SQL and DAX
•    Knowledge and experience with Databricks/Analytics Studio, preferably with a licence
•    Fluent in English
We also like you to:
•    Understand the importance of good stakeholder management
•    Take ownership and focus on high quality work/code
•    Enjoy working in a multicultural environment
We are offering
The success of our organisation depends on the quality of our people and the ideas that they have. Care, Collaboration and Courage are our key values. Truly surprising insights and innovative solutions for our clients result from an interplay of cultures, knowledge and experience. Diversity is therefore extremely important to our organisation. To ensure that everyone at ABN AMRO can develop their talents, we encourage an inclusive culture in which all colleagues feel engaged and appreciated.
We’re offering:
•    The opportunity to be the best you can be and lots of room to grow both personally and professionally
•    Possibilities for flexible working hours and work from home are negotiable. We offer good facilities for remote working
•    A personal development budget of EUR 1.000 per year, chances to visit tech seminars and events
•    An annual public transportation pass or travel budget, depending on the function
•    Utilization of the latest technology and advanced tooling in a well-equipped office
•    Large responsibility and freedom to finish your tasks
Interested?
Please express your interest by applying online with your resume and a (short) letter in which you explain your interest.
You can apply until 30 April. After this date, the vacancy will close and you will receive feedback on your application shortly.
Your Future: Inclusive, Innovative, Sustainable
At ABN AMRO, we believe in "
Banking for better, for generations to come
." Equal opportunities for everyone are a crucial foundation, as we strive for an inclusive culture where all employees feel seen, heard, and valued. Our vision of being a personal bank in the digital age aligns perfectly with the demand for surprising insights and innovative solutions, born from a diverse interplay of cultures and experiences. We focus on customer experience, sustainability, and building a future-proof bank, conducting annual reviews to ensure equal pay for equal work. Join a bank that embraces ingenuity and ambition, and make an impact with us for a better future.
If you want to apply for the B-Able or Reboot program, make sure to mention it both in the title and the content of your resume.

title: Software Engineering Manager – Unified Platform
description:
This is Adyen
Adyen provides payments, data, and financial products in a single solution for customers like Meta, Uber, H&M, and Microsoft — making us the financial technology platform of choice. At Adyen, everything we do is engineered for ambition.
For our teams, we create an environment with opportunities for our people to succeed, backed by the culture and support to ensure they are enabled to truly own their careers. We are motivated individuals who tackle unique technical challenges at scale and solve them as a team. Together, we deliver innovative and ethical solutions that help businesses achieve their ambitions faster.
Why you should join the Unified Platform
The
Unified Platform
powers the core financial infrastructure behind Adyen’s platform - from how transactions are processed to how financial data is stored, monitored, and configured. It plays a critical role in ensuring that payments, accounting, reporting, and reconciliation work seamlessly across all products and customer segments.
We are currently hiring
four Software Engineering Managers
within the Unified Platform. These teams work on different parts of the platform but share a common mission: building scalable, reliable systems that power Adyen’s global financial ecosystem.
Depending on your experience and strengths, you may join one of the following domains:
Transaction Platform
Build and support Adyen’s core transaction platform by providing a highly available API to store and retrieve transaction data and events across the system.
Storage & Scale
Design and evolve the storage architecture behind Adyen’s financial systems, ensuring our data infrastructure can scale reliably as transaction volumes grow.
Finance Observability
Enable transparent and reliable financial operations by building monitoring and alerting systems for critical financial flows across the platform.
Unified Configuration
Create a consistent platform experience by standardizing internal infrastructure and configuration systems that power Adyen’s financial products.
Your final team placement will be determined based on your skills, experience, and the technical fit identified during the interview process.
What you’ll do
Lead, grow, and mentor a team of 5–6 engineers building critical components of Adyen’s financial infrastructure.
Guide the design and development of scalable distributed systems using Core Java, web services, queueing frameworks, REST APIs, and SQL/PostgreSQL.
Collaborate closely with product and platform teams to design systems that are reliable, scalable, and easy to operate.
Balance long-term technical improvements with product delivery.
Foster a culture of ownership, collaboration, and continuous improvement.
Set clear team goals aligned with engineering and product strategy.
Improve operational excellence through automation, observability, and system reliability.
Hire and develop engineering talent that thrives in a fast-moving environment.
Who you are
A strong people manager who enjoys coaching engineers and enabling teams to perform at their best.
Technically proficient in backend engineering, with strong experience in Java or other modern programming languages used to build large-scale distributed systems.
Comfortable making difficult decisions and communicating clearly with strong senior engineers.
Skilled at collaborating across teams and aligning stakeholders around technical decisions.
A clear communicator who can explain complex technical concepts to both engineers and non-technical audiences.
Experienced working with senior leadership to shape technical strategy and roadmaps.
Minimum qualifications
3+ years of people management experience
leading engineering teams.
8+ years of experience in backend software engineering.

title: Data Engineer
description:
This is Adyen
Adyen provides payments, data, and financial products in a single solution for customers like Meta, Uber, H&M, and Microsoft - making us the financial technology platform of choice. At Adyen, everything we do is engineered for ambition.
For our teams, we create an environment with opportunities for our people to succeed, backed by the culture and support to ensure they are enabled to truly own their careers. We are motivated individuals who tackle unique technical challenges at scale and solve them as a team. Together, we deliver innovative and ethical solutions that help businesses achieve their ambitions faster.
Data Engineer
At Adyen, we treat data and data artifacts as first-class citizens. They form our backbone and drive the business and product insights we generate. To this end, Adyen is looking for a Data Engineer II to join our team in Amsterdam. This role requires a solid understanding of both the business context and its data needs, with a focus on building high-quality data pipelines on our Big Data Platform. As a Data Engineer at Adyen, you will be instrumental in maintaining our data infrastructure and ensuring smooth data flows across systems.
What you’ll do
Collaborative Solution Development
: Engage with a diverse range of stakeholders, including data scientists, analysts, software engineers, product managers, and customers, to understand their requirements and craft effective solutions.
Quality Pipelines and Architecture
: Design, develop, deploy and operate high-quality production ELT pipelines and data architectures. Integrate data from various sources and formats, ensuring compatibility, consistency, and reliability.
Data Best Practices
: Help establish and share best practices in performance, code quality, data validation, data governance, and discoverability in your team and in other teams. Participate in mentoring and knowledge sharing initiatives.
High Quality Data and Code:
Ensure data is accurate, complete, reliable, relevant, and timely. Implement testing, monitoring and validation protocols for your code and data, leveraging tools such as Pytest.
Performance Optimization
: Identify and resolve performance bottlenecks in data pipelines and systems. Improve query performance and resource utilization to meet SLAs and performance requirements, using technologies Spark optimizations.
Who you are
Experienced in Big Data:
3+ years
of experience working as a Data Engineer or in a similar role.
Data & Engineering practices:
You have a solid understanding of both Software and Data Engineering practices.
Technical Super Star:
Proficient in tools and languages such as: Python, PySpark, Airflow, Hadoop, Spark, Kafka, SQL, Git.
Clear Communicator:
Able to effectively communicate complex data-related concepts and outcomes to a diverse range of stakeholders.
Self-starter:
Capable of identifying opportunities, devising solutions, and handling projects independently.
Innovator:
You have an experimental mindset with a ‘launch fast and iterate’ mentality.
Data Culture Champion:

title: Medior Data Scientist (Python, Azure, Databricks, Spark)
description:
.
Your day is done—you’ve pushed your code, and it’s already been reviewed and merged. Now it’s time to grab some groceries on the way home. Walking through the refrigerated aisles of your local Albert Heijn store, you spot a great bargain—salad leaves marked down to 70%, thanks to a dynamic discount algorithm you helped develop, ensuring products sell before expiration and avoiding waste.
As you walk towards the bakery department, the aroma of freshly baked goods fills the air. You take pride in knowing that your optimized baking plan is behind the freshness and quality of these products. Stepping out onto the street you realize that it’s not just your local store—these algorithms run in more than a thousand stores across the Netherlands and Belgium.
Does a job with such tangible impact at such scale motivates you? Do you want to use your skills to solve a wide set of practical and challenging problems? We’re looking for curious, collaborative people who care about impact and learning to join and strengthen our Operations Analytics and Data Science team!
As Medior Data Scientist you:
Improve our algorithms through analysis, modelling and experimentation
Design, build, test, and productionize algorithms in collaboration with engineers and analysts
Work on ETL pipelines to make sure we work with up-to-date and correct data
Convey complex ideas to different audiences in an effective manner
Work in multidisciplinary product teams closely to our business stakeholders
Share your knowledge with and learn from other team members
Work with colleagues who value open discussion, constructive code reviews, and diverse perspectives.
We’re looking for candidates with:
A Master in Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Econometrics, Computer Science, or other relevant studies
Knowledge of Machine Learning and Applied Statistical techniques
Strong knowledge of Python
2-5 years of relevant experience
Experience using modern software development practices (IDEs, Unit testing, Version Control, CI/CD, etc.)
Experience with generative AI–assisted coding (GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, or similar) and the ability to prompt effectively, assess suggestions critically, and uphold security and quality standards.
Experience with Cloud Platforms, Databricks and Spark is a plus
Don’t meet every single requirement? If you’re excited about this role, we encourage you to apply.
You can also read more about some of the technical details of our work on our blog:
https://blog.ah.technology
What we offer:
A salary up to €75K gross per year, including holiday allowance (8%). On top of that you will receive a profit share
25,5 vacation days
A budget choice in which you can decide whether you spend it on extra days off (+19,5 days), discounts or pay-outs
Courses from our Albert Heijn TOP Academy
Hybrid working (2–3 days in the office) and flexible hours
An excellent pension plan with a personal contribution of 6.2%
A travel allowance for traveling to and from Zaandam
Attractive discounts on various insurance policies
A MacBook
and an iPhone
10% discount on your grocery shopping in our Albert Heijn stores, up to €300 per year
We find it important that you can be yourself at Albert Heijn in an environment that is open to different perspectives, an environment where we are curious and appreciate each other.
We’re Albert Heijn, food retail reinventors.
🚀
Albert Heijn goes way back. More than 135 years, to be exact. So for millions of people, food shopping is synonymous with the Albert Heijn brand. We didn’t achieve this with a complacent, ‘hey, if it works today, it’ll work tomorrow’ approach, but by continually rethinking our propositions and staying plugged in to customer demands. More than 125,000 colleagues? Check. Over 1,250 stores in the Netherlands and Flanders? Check. The leading online food retail platform? Check, we build, run and love it.
Together we make eating better the easy choice. For everyone.
The world around us has changed, and significantly at that. These changes have impact on the lives of our customers, colleagues and partners. And therefore, also on Albert Heijn. We’re moving towards a new world with new opportunities and challenges. Albert Heijn wants more people to eat fresher, healthier and more sustainable food. Eating brings us together, keeps us healthier and contributes positively to the world. Our mission is: Together we make eating better the easy choice. For everyone.
Albert Heijn the Netherlands is B Corp certified. This underscores our purpose, through which we aim to contribute to a healthier, more social, and sustainable society. B Corp certification is a designation that a business is meeting high standards of verified performance, accountability and transparency.
Ready to contribute to our mission?
Do you want to play an active role in changing the food retail landscape as we know it? Then hop on board and join us on our expedition. Would you like to learn more first, before you apply to Albert Heijn? We’re happy to tell you everything about this vacancy! An assessment might be part of our selection process.
Apply
Do you want to play an active role in changing the food retail landscape as we know it? Then hop on board and join us on our expedition. Apply via the button on this page. We ask you not to send a standard motivation letter, but to formulate an answer to the following questions:
Why do you think you will be successful in this position and what kind of technical skills do you bring with you for this role?
What is one of your personal strengths and one skill you wish to improve or develop?
Want to know more before you apply for our role at AH Technology? Peter Dirks, Talent Acquisition Partner Data Science & Analytics, is happy to tell you everything about this vacancy via peter.dirks@ah.nl
.

title: Netwerk Engineer - Den Haag / Hybride - 32-40u
description:
Working at TNO means contributing to research and innovative applications that matter. From the energy transition and safe AI applications in healthcare, to the future of digital identity and our security. As a Network Engineer, your challenge is to manage complex networks that enable over 4,500 TNO employees at 20 locations in the Netherlands to carry out their daily work and research. You have an eye for detail, security, and can perform coordinating tasks.
As a Network Engineer, you are responsible for ensuring the availability and maintenance of the network infrastructure. This includes, among other things, the following tasks and responsibilities:
Ensuring adequate performance and system security.
Conducting performance measurements and trend analyses, and making system adjustments.
Managing and administering changes.
Advising on the urgency, impact, and priority of changes to be implemented.
Keeping up with the latest techniques and trends in the field.
Developing ideas to further improve the department’s service quality.
Maintaining operational contacts with software and hardware suppliers.
Because TNO conducts a lot of technological research for Defence, the secure availability of our networks is crucial, both for TNO and for society.
The Team
As a Network Engineer, you are part of the Infrastructure team. This team is responsible for the data centers, storage environment, networks, (Linux and Windows) servers, and the basic setup of clients. The team is also responsible for IAAS and PAAS cloud services and integration with on-premise services. The team primarily works from the location The Hague Nieuw Babylon and secondarily from locations in Central and Southern Netherlands. They work in a hybrid manner.
To perform well in this role, it is important that you recognize yourself in the following profile:
At least higher professional education (HBO) working and thinking level.
Preferably demonstrable experience as a LAN/WAN Network Engineer in a relevant work environment.
Demonstrable experience with Cisco, Palo Alto, F5, Infoblox.
Able to optimize and automate management tasks.
A flexible, customer- and service-oriented attitude.
Analytical and problem-solving skills.
Good command of Dutch and English.
In possession of a category B driving license.
Challenging and varied work with a real impact. And plenty of opportunities as, at TNO, you are in charge of shaping your career. We offer a gross monthly salary between € 3.450,- and € 5.250,- (based on your knowledge and experience), 8% holiday pay, a 13th month bonus of 8.33% and a flex budget (5.58% + € 180). In total, your salary including all this can range between € 4.000 and € 6.000 per month. In addition, you will be given every opportunity to develop yourself.
TNO offers optional employee benefits, enabling you to tailor your benefits package to match your personal situation. You may also expect:
An extremely professional, innovative working environment where colleagues are leading experts in their field.
The opportunity to attend courses, workshops and conferences, and to receive training and coaching based on your needs.
33 days annual leave on a full-time basis.

mobility plan

title: Senior Enterprise Architect | The Hague
description:
Working at TNO means contributing to research and innovative applications that truly matter. Examples include the energy transition, the application of safe AI in healthcare, the future of digital identity, and our overall security. As an Enterprise Architect, your challenge is to ensure—at a corporate level—the development, expansion, replacement, and maintenance of the many complex networks and IT infrastructures that enable more than 5,000 TNO employees, across 20 locations in the Netherlands (and beyond), to carry out their daily work and research.
As a Senior Enterprise Architect, you are—together with six direct colleagues—responsible for developing, promoting, and safeguarding TNO’s enterprise architecture. You act as a strategic advisor to management and help the organization make future-proof choices in the areas of information management and IT.
You work closely with a wide range of TNO colleagues, such as architects, project managers, product owners, security officers, and stakeholders within TNO’s research and support departments.
Examples of typical activities in this role:
Developing and maintaining the enterprise architecture vision and roadmaps, in line with TNO’s strategy.
Advising the Executive Board, senior leadership, and management on strategic IT and information-related issues.
Assessing programs and projects against architectural principles and frameworks.
Translating organizational and research objectives into concrete architectural decisions.
Safeguarding coherence between business, data, applications, and infrastructure within a technologically diverse landscape.
Contributing to the further development of themes such as Generative AI, data quality, strategic autonomy, and software development, as well as supporting projects and programs that realize these themes.
Actively contributing to architecture governance and the professionalization of architecture within TNO.
Inspiring colleagues in the field of architecture and strategic thinking.
Academic level of work and thinking (minimum completed HBO/BSc degree).
At least 8–10 years of relevant experience in architecture roles, preferably as an Enterprise Architect within a complex organization.
Proven experience with enterprise architecture frameworks (such as TOGAF or comparable).
Extensive knowledge of information management, IT landscapes, data architecture, and cloud concepts.
Experience working in a governance‑complex, knowledge‑intensive environment.
Strong advisory skills and the ability to bridge strategy and execution (translating abstract concepts into practical application and implementation).
Excellent communication skills and persuasive ability.
Proactive mindset, strong analytical capabilities, and organizational sensitivity.
Excellent command of both Dutch and English, verbally and in writing.

title: Cloud en Distributed Platforms Engineer | Groningen
description:
As a Distributed Computing & Cloud-native Engineer, you don’t just build systems—you help shape the future of how Europe handles data and computing at scale. From AI to edge computing, from cloud-native platforms to energy-aware infrastructure: at TNO, you’ll work on complex challenges with real societal impact.
You’ll be part of a multidisciplinary team at the crossroads of distributed computing and cloud-native engineering. Together, we develop future-proof computing platforms that are sovereign, federated, , and sustainable. Think: hybrid architectures that power smart energy grids, autonomous systems, or AI-driven logistics. You’ll prototype, test, and refine next-generation platforms where edge computing, cloud orchestration, HPC, and alternative computing paradigms meet.
Your work will contribute to impactful initiatives in sectors such as artificial intelligence, sustainable energy, logistics, and public infrastructure. You will work on the design and implementation of distributed architectures tailored for scalable, fault-tolerant, and energy-conscious applications. From the very first prototype to the final deployment, you’ll work closely with researchers and engineers to translate innovative concepts into operational systems.
Some of your core activities:
Build distributed architectures for real-world scenarios with scalability, resilience, and energy-efficiency in mind.
Engineer cloud-native and edge-native services that support digital twins and autonomous decision-making.
Apply DevOps practices for smooth, secure, and reliable deployment cycles.
You’ll also contribute to research-driven infrastructure development and actively collaborate with stakeholders in academia and industry.
You’re not just a systems engineer. You’re someone who gets energy from working in a collaborative environment and enjoys turning complex ideas into real-world solutions. You like experimenting, building prototypes, and learning from every iteration. You’re curious, driven, and not afraid to ask ‘what if…?’.
Next to this, you also have:
A MSc or PhD in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Embedded Systems or similar.
Hands-on experience with distributed systems and orchestration tooling (e.g., Kubernetes, Docker).
Solid programming skills in one or more of: Python, Go, Rust, Scala, Java.
Familiarity with real-world system-of-systems engineering (e.g., Linux-based environments, CI/CD, infrastructure-as-code tools like Terraform).
A strong understanding of both cloud (IaaS, PaaS) and edge computing, including concepts like smart control, federation, and technical interoperability.
Bonus points if you also have experience in:
Understanding of modern machine learning workflows.
Experience with energy-aware computing or sustainable IT.
Knowledge of Digital Twin platforms or industrial control systems.
Affinity with alternative computing models (WASM, quantum simulators, event-driven services).
Familiarity with cloud services like AWS, Azure, or GCP.
Challenging and varied work with a real impact. And plenty of opportunities as, at TNO, you are in charge of shaping your career. We offer a gross monthly salary between € 3.385,- and € 5.000,- (based on your knowledge and experience), 8% holiday pay, a 13th month bonus of 8.33% and a flex budget (5.58% + € 180). In addition, you will be given every opportunity to develop yourself.

title: Projectmanager ICT | The Hague or Groningen
description:
A powerful digital future in The Netherlands and Europe is unthinkable without research and innovation. Fortunately, TNO is bursting with exiting project plans, which, thanks to you, will actually come to fruition. As a project manager ICT at TNO, you will work at the cutting edge of applied research and pragmatic ICT solutions for the energy domain. Join our enthusiastic group of ICT specialists and take the lead in project teams. Together with companies, universities, research centres and government partners, you’ll help resolve energy transition issues faced by industry and society as a whole!
You will manage multiple research projects simultaneously, ranging from B2B consultancy assignments to multi-year, grant-funded TKI projects (Top Consortia for Knowledge and Innovation). Especially in consortium projects, stakeholder management is key due to the diverse—and sometimes conflicting—interests at play. You safeguard the shared objectives and the overall progress of the project.
Together with your multidisciplinary team, you will develop innovative proof-of-concepts and demonstrators. Your teams work on topics such as modelling of distributed, hybrid energy systems, Digital Twins, adaptive algorithms, (agentic) AI, and more.
Your focus will initially be on Dutch projects, but depending on your interests, you’ll have the chance to move on to European projects.
You will be part of the Intelligent Cyber-physical Systems Engineering (ICSE) research group. From our locations in Groningen and The Hague, more than 35 scientists, researchers, consultants and project managers demonstrate the added value of digital technologies every day. You will also work closely with colleagues across other departments and units.
ICSE offers a vibrant mix of highly educated professionals of different ages, nationalities, genders and experience levels. What unites us is our passion for applied research and our love for an open, informal working atmosphere. And at TNO you’re never on your own: we support each other, collaborate closely and welcome your ideas.
You thrive on managing multidisciplinary research projects. The inherent uncertainties of innovative projects—with outcomes yet unknown—don’t throw you off balance. Inspiring people, coming up with creative solutions and working together are second nature to you.
With your strong communication skills, you’re able to convince your team, clients, partners and other stakeholders. You navigate sensitivities and differing interests with ease. And of course, you do this within budget, timeline and quality standards. If something doesn’t go according to plan, you raise the flag early.
You also bring the following knowledge and skills to TNO:
A Master’s degree or PhD in computer science or a related field.
A couple of years (3-5 years) work experience, including some on project management.
Excellent command of both Dutch (C1 level) and English, as you'll work with international teams and Dutch clients.
Preferably experience with IPMA or other project management methodologies.
Demonstrable knowledge and experience in ICT and/or the energy transition is a plus.
Challenging and varied work with a real impact. And plenty of opportunities as, at TNO, you are in charge of shaping your career. We offer a gross monthly salary between € 4.700,- and € 6.800,- (based on your knowledge and experience), 8% holiday pay, a 13th month bonus of 8.33% and a flex budget (5.58% + € 180). In addition, you will be given every opportunity to develop yourself.
TNO offers optional employee benefits, enabling you to tailor your benefits package to match your personal situation. You may also expect:
An extremely professional, innovative working environment where colleagues are leading experts in their field.
The opportunity to attend courses, workshops and conferences, and to receive training and coaching based on your needs.
33 days annual leave on a full-time basis plus a flexible holiday (prorated) that can be used on a cultural, religious, or otherwise important day of your choosing.
An employer that values and encourages diverse talent, with initiatives like the Female Leadership Program, our Rainbow Community and round tables on inclusion topics.
We offer a comprehensive and flexible
mobility plan
that also includes full compensation for public transportation for commuting and business travel.
Great social events with your team and other
TNO colleagues
. That’s how you will get to know a lot of people really quickly.
Flexible working hours, the possibility to work parttime (32 or 36 hours) and the possibility of working from home.
A good pension scheme.
Read
more
about tailoring your benefits package.
At TNO, we innovate for a healthier, safer and more sustainable life. And for a strong economy. Since 1932, we have been making knowledge and technology available for the common good. We find each other in wonder and ingenuity. We are driven to push boundaries. There is all the space and support for your talent and ambition. You work with people who will challenge you: who inspire you and want to learn from you. Our state-of-the-art facilities are there to realize your vision. What you do at TNO matters: impact makes the difference. Because with every innovation you contribute to tomorrow’s world. Read more about
TNO as an employer
.
At TNO we encourage an inclusive work environment, where you can be yourself. Whatever your story and whatever unique qualities you bring to the table. It is by combining our unique strengths and perspectives that we are able to develop innovations that make a real difference in society. Want to know more? Read what steps we are taking in the area of
diversity, equity and inclusion
.
Please apply before the 17th of May, 2026. The
selection process
comprises two interview rounds. The preliminary interview takes place on the 27th of May, 2026. The second interview round has been scheduled for the 4th of May, 2026. In a final meeting we will discuss the terms of employment and your tailored benefits package. We aim to finalize the entire process within four weeks.
The selection process may include an online assessment and a reference check.
A certificate of Conduct (VOG) is required before starting a new job at TNO.
Then we’d like to hear from you! Please contact us for more information about the job or the selection process. To apply, please upload your CV and covering letter using the ‘apply now’ button.
